taxonomy: Malacocarpus molendensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or bean family. The genus Malacocarpus belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. Malacocarpus molendensis is native to South Africa, where it grows in a variety of habitats. Its classification is further supported by its anatomical and morphological characteristics, which are typical of plants in the Fabaceae family. As a member of this family, Malacocarpus molendensis is closely related to other plants such as Acacia and Crotalaria.
